person.name: Jeanne de BOURBON
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Jeanne de Bourbon-Clermont se désista de ses prétentions sur le Forez en faveur de sa petite-fille, à condition de jouir do revenus considérables et du château de Clcppé (1376), qui, avec Saint-Galmier, était sa résidence préférée.
elle se fit du moins céder les châteaux de Chambéon et Donzy au contre, du Verdier et de Vernay au nord, Châtelneuf à l'ouest. Plus tard, elle réussit à le supplanter et se fit aussitôt faire par son fils la donation même du comté (juin 1368).
Louis H, duc do Bourbon, provoquait un conseil de famille dont no faisaient pas partie la mère ni l'oncle du comte, et où la tutelle lui fut déférée (1368). Peu de temps après, il .épousa la nièce de Louis et de Jean H, Anne d'Auvergne, fille de Jeanne de Forez et du dauphin d'Auvergne (1369). Des deux tuteurs dépossédés, Renaud de Malleval mourut peu de temps après, mais Jeanne persista deux ans dans la lutte contre le mari de sa petite-fille, enfin, un accord intervint en 1370, leur assurant l'exercice en commun de l'autorité.

Ces rivalités de famille entre une mère, un oncle, un neveu par alliance, autour d'un prince insensé, du vivant de qui ils déchirèrent son héritage, ont une singulière ressemblance avec ce qu'on rencontre vingt ans après dans l'histoire de la royauté : Charles VI, imbécile comme Jean II, et dont les proches : ses oncles, sa femme et son frère, se disputent le pouvoir.

Au milieu de ces divisions et de ces pertes, la seigneurie acquise auprès de Crémone, en Italie, et déjà réduite à peu près à une vainc prétention, est oubliée et disparaît.

La mère succéda à son fils, comme comtesse de Forez, en 1372. Mais le duc de Bourbon ayant désintéressé le duc d'Anjou n'en fut qu'un plus incommode compétiteur, et exerça véritablement l'autorité (1373).

C'est ainsi que le Forez passa à une troisième dynastie, celle des Capétiens-Bourbons, dans la personne de Louis II le Bon.
Sources :
-Histoire du Forez par Antoine Benjamin
relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon Mangas

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne de BOURBON於大約1312年出生時，她的父親Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame34歲，母親Marie d'Avesnes34歲。她於1324年與Guigues VII de Forez結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1402年12月30日享年91歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: BOURBON
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from a village in Allier, the site of the (now ruined) castle of Bourbon, or from another place called (Le) Bourbon, mainly in the southern part of France. The placename is derived from a Celtic and pre-Celtic element borb- denoting a hot spring.

History: The progenitor of a large part of the American bearers of the surname Bourbon was Jean-Pierre Bourbon from Salins-les-Bains in Jura, France, who settled in MO in the middle of the 19th century. However, many bearers of the surname claim a connection with the house of Bourbon, which provided generations of monarchs of France and Spain and other European royalty. It takes its name from the castle of Bourbon in Allier, which was held by Adhémar, a 9th-century noble. His descendant Beatrice, heiress of Bourbon, married Robert of Clermont, 6th son of King Louis IX of France, in 1272, and these two are considered founders of the royal house. Robert's son Louis was created Duke of Bourbon in 1327. By the 16th century they had added much of southern France, the dukedom of Vendôme, and the kingdom of Navarre to their fiefs, and in 1589 a Bourbon succeeded to the throne of France as Henry IV. His grandson, Louis XIV, called ‘the Sun King’, reigned 1643–1715, presiding over a golden age of French literature and art and attempting to establish French supremacy in Europe. The Bourbons ruled France until the Revolution in 1793 and again 1815–48. The present claimants to the French throne are descended from Louis XIV's brother, Philippe, Duke of Orléans. The Bourbons acquired the Spanish throne in 1700, when a grandson of Louis XIV succeeded as Philip V; they ruled until 1931 and were restored, in the person of King Juan Carlos, in 1975. The Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies (i.e. Sicily and Naples), 1759–1861, and Bourbon dukes of Parma, 1748–1860, were descended from the Spanish branch of the family.

variants.name: Borbon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ana, Jesus, Marta, Miguel Angel, Tomas, Ademar, Alfredo, Ana Maria, Bernabe, Berta, Carlos.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne de BOURBON was born about 1312, her father, Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame, was 34 and her mother, Marie d'Avesnes, was 34. She married Guigues VII de Forez in 1324. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 30 December 1402, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne de BOURBON est née environ 1312, son père, Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Marie d'Avesnes, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Guigues VII de Forez en 1324. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 30 décembre 1402, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne de BOURBON um 1312 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame 34 und ihre Mutter Marie d'Avesnes 34. Sie hat 1324 Guigues VII de Forez geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 30. Dezember 1402 im Alter von 91 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de BOURBON è nata il circa 1312, sua padre, Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Marie d'Avesnes, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Guigues VII de Forez nel 1324. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 30 dicembre 1402, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne de BOURBONが約1312年に生まれた時，父Louis De Bourbon I,The Lameは34歳，母Marie d'Avesnesは34歳であった。1324年にGuigues VII de Forezと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1402年12月30日に91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne de BOURBON(이)가 대략 1312년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie d'Avesnes(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1324년 Guigues VII de Forez(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1402년 12월 30일 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne de BOURBON nasceu aproximadamente 1312, seu pai, Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Marie d'Avesnes, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Guigues VII de Forez em 1324. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 30 de dezembro de 1402, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne de BOURBON родилась около 1312 г., её отцу по имени Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Marie d'Avesnes, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guigues VII de Forez в 1324 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 30 декабря 1402 г. в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne de BOURBON nació aproximadamente 1312, su papá, Louis De Bourbon I,The Lame, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Marie d'Avesnes, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Guigues VII de Forez en 1324. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 30 de diciembre de 1402, a la edad de 91 años.
